text: Old friendships are easy to neglect.
Life gets busy, and before you know it, months have passed since you last spoke.
The problem is rarely a lack of loyalty — it is simply that daily tasks take over.
Yet the people who knew us years ago hold something valuable: a mutual understanding that newer friends may not share.
Keeping in touch does not require a grand gesture.
A short message, a photo from a place you both remember, or a quick voice call can be enough.
What matters is that the effort feels genuine, not forced.
Some people linger in our thoughts without us ever reaching out.
There is a common assumption that they are too busy or that too much time has passed.
But most of the time, they are waiting for the same thing we are — a simple sign that somebody still cares.
Do not let an old friendship fade.
Embrace the awkwardness of reconnecting, and you may find that the bond is stronger than you thought.
